channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: EU supports AI challenge to Silicon Valley and China
item.description: The European Union is backing a major AI initiative, OpenEuroLLM, in a bid to counter the dominance of Silicon Valley and China in artificial intelligence. The initiative is being led by Jan Hajič, a computational linguist at Charles University in Czechia, and Peter Sarlin, co-founder of Silo AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Globe Life data breach may have affected 850,000 more patients than previously thought
item.description: Healthcare company initially said cyberattack affected 5,000 people, but stressed the number is likely bigger.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Has the rush to upgrade to Windows 11 just begun? New stats show a marked uptick as Windows 10 End of Life looms later in 2025
item.description: Windows 10 is being retired in October 2025, so it’s no surprise that more and more people are now jumping ship to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra seemingly scratches more easily than its predecessor
item.description: A durability test has revealed that the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra's scratch resistance might have had a downgrade.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The DeepSeek lesson
item.description: During the past two weeks, DeepSeek unraveled Silicon Valley’s comfortable narrative about generative artificial intelligence by introducing dramatically more efficient ways to scale large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tetragon: Extending eBPF and Cilium to runtime security
item.description: If you’re responsible for security, you’d better be good at context switching. Enterprise infrastructure has never been more distributed, and the software stack has never had more moving parts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IDC and e& enterprise to lead AI Innovation at 18th annual Middle East CIO Summit
item.description: The 18th Annual IDC Middle East CIO Summit is set to explore the dynamic world of artificial intelligence and its potential to drive business success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: De TI a RRHH: Cómo la tecnología está influyendo en los equipos humanos
item.description: Hace tres décadas, la relación entre el equipo de recursos humanos y el de TI se limitaba a que este último arreglase el ordenador o instalase algún programa al primero. Pero estas tres décadas han sido de un desarrollo tecnológico acelerado, que ha cambiado todo el paradigma empresarial.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Multiple tremors near Greek island of Santorini shut schools and put residents on edge
item.description: A series of earthquakes near the Greek island of Santorini have led authorities to shut down schools, dispatch rescue teams with sniffer dogs and sent instructions to residents including a request to drain their swimming pools.
item.dc:creator: Demetris Nellas, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: Texas ordered a ban on the Chinese AI startup DeepSeek on government devices days after the popular chatbot has shook investors and the tech community in recent weeks for its ability to compete with OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple reports dip in iPhone sales over the holidays, despite AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Italy blocked AI app DeepSeek in Apple and Google stores. Here’s why
item.description: The Chinese artificial intelligence app DeepSeek could not be accessed on Wednesday in Apple and Google app stores in Italy, the day after the country’s data protection authority requested information on its use of personal data.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI unleashes o3-mini reasoning model
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released the latest model in its reasoning series, o3-mini, both in ChatGPT and its application programming interface (API). It had been in preview since December 2024.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump vows sweeping tariffs on oil, pharmaceuticals and semiconductors
item.description: President Trump on Friday vowed to impose sweeping tariffs on semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, steel and aluminum and oil and gas. "We'll be doing pharmaceuticals. Importantly in drugs and medicines, etc. All forms of medicine and pharmaceuticals," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: VMware offers fixes to severe vulnerabilities in VMware Aria
item.description: VMware has fixed multiple high-severity vulnerabilities affecting its cloud management platform (CMP), VMware Aria, which could allow attackers to steal sensitive credentials from the virtualization giant’s IT management and logging solutions.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: The quantum computing reality check
item.description: In late 2024, a major pharmaceutical company invested $50 million in quantum computing initiatives through a leading cloud provider, hoping to revolutionize their drug discovery process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: If you were bored watching videos at a plodding 2x speed, you’re in luck: They can go faster
item.description: MrBeast’s fast-cutting, nonstop dramatics are already quick enough. Now, some users can watch his videos (and others) at 4x speed. YouTube is continuing its push for pace. In a new experiment, Premium users will be able to quadruple the speed of its videos.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Grim memories resurface after DNA advances lead to arrest of suspect in Hawaii teen's 1977 murder
item.description: The unsolved death of a teenager in 1977 haunted students and staff at Honolulu's McKinley High School for nearly half a century.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sourcegraph unveils AI coding agents
item.description: Looking to challenge well-established GitHub Copilot in AI-powered coding assistance, Sourcegraph has announced coding agents for code review, code migration, testing, documentation, and notification, along with an API for building custom agents. Sourcegraph made the announcement on January 29.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 exceptional text expansion tools for Windows
item.description: Do you find yourself typing the same things over and over? If the answer is yes — and if you type enough text during the workweek, it almost certainly will be — I’d highly recommend you install a text expansion application.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The biggest ideas in software and technology today
item.description: One of the coolest things about being a software developer is that you often know what’s behind the curtain. To some extent, at least.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Now US government agencies can use OpenAI’s ChatGPT too
item.description: Everyone wants to get their hands on AI tools, and employees in government agencies are no exception. Still, stakes are far higher for governments when it comes to security, privacy, and protecting the public.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: UK regulator may investigate AWS and Microsoft cloud businesses
item.description: AWS and Microsoft could potentially face an investigation of their cloud businesses in Britain should the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) follow the recommendation of an independent inquiry group that reports into the UK’s antitrust regulator.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Unknown threat actor targeting Juniper routers with backdoor: Report
item.description: Network administrators using routers from Juniper Networks are being urged to scan for possible compromise after the discovery that an unknown threat actor has been installing a backdoor in customer routers since at least 2023.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: EY’s own experience with AI provides important lessons
item.description: Anja Allen, a principal at EY, said the internal rollout of Microsoft Copilot offered insights into what goes into successful AI implementations. The post EY’s own experience with AI provides important lessons first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system
item.description: The plaintiffs claim OPM violated the E-Government Act by not releasing details of how the email system will manage federal employees’ personal information. The post Federal employee lawsuit alleges security, privacy risks in new OPM communications system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Networking terms and definitions
item.description: To find a brief definition of the networking term you are looking for user your browser’s “Find” feature then follow links to a fuller explanation. AI networking AI networking refers to the application of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies to network management and optimization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Industry Exchange Cyber 2025: Tanium’s Melissa Bischoping on why autonomous is new watchword in endpoint security
item.description: Tanium product research director describes autonomous endpoint security as akin to techie “colleague” that can help cyber team keep ahead of threats.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM Unveils Enterprise Data Sets for AI Development on Mainframes
item.description: IBM launches a portfolio of enterprise data sets aimed at jumpstarting AI development for its Z and LinuxONE mainframe customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: DeepSeek's new AI chatbot and ChatGPT answer sensitive questions about China differently
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Chinese tech startup DeepSeek ’s new artificial intelligence chatbot has sparked discussions about the competition between China and the U.S. in AI development, with many users flocking to test the rival of OpenAI's ChatGPT. DeepSeek’s AI assistant was the No.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Upstart Chinese AI company DeepSeek's founder started out as a low-key hedge fund entrepreneur
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The 40-year-old founder of China’s DeepSeek, an AI startup that has startled markets with its capacity to compete with industry leaders like OpenAI, kept a low profile as he built up a hedge fund and then refined its quantitative models to branch into artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Modernizing Federal Human Resources Systems for Efficiency
item.description: The new presidential administration aims to find efficiencies in government by modernizing human resources systems, leading to cost savings and improved user experience.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network
